latest.title: Exercise of Warrants, Issue of Equity and TVR
latest.content:
Today 08:21
RNS Number : 4624I
IQE PLC
16 June 2026
IQE plc
Cardiff, UK
16 June 2026
Exercise of Warrants, Issue of Equity and TVR
IQE plc (AIM: IQE, "IQE" or the "Group"),
the leading global supplier of compound
semiconductor wafer products and advanced material solutions, announces that it has received notices of warrant exercise in respect of 5,864,624 ordinary shares of 1 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") in the capital of the Company at an exercise price of 13.5 pence per share.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for Admission of the 5,864,624 Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective on or around 17 June 2026.
The Ordinary Shares will be issued fully paid and will rank pari passu in all respects with the Company's existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission, the total number of Ordinary Shares in the capital of the Company in issue will be 1,320,656,252 with voting rights. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company's issued share capital.

ABOUT IQE
http://iqep.com
IQE is the leading global supplier of advanced compound semiconductor wafers and materials solutions that enable a diverse range of applications across:
·
Smart Connected Devices
·
Communications Infrastructure
·
Automotive and Industrial
·
Aerospace and Security
As a scaled global epitaxy wafer manufacturer, IQE is uniquely positioned in this market which has high barriers to entry. IQE supplies the global market and is enabling customers to innovate at chip and OEM level. By leveraging the Group's intellectual property portfolio including know-how and patents, it produces epitaxy wafers of superior quality, yield and unit economics.
IQE is headquartered in Cardiff UK, with employees across manufacturing locations in the UK, US and Taiwan, and is listed on the AIM Stock Exchange in London.

latest.title: IQE plc: Tower Semiconductor InP supply agreement
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1844I
IQE PLC
15 June 2026
IQE plc
Cardiff, UK
15 June 2026
IQE and Tower Semiconductor announce multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement
-
Supporting planned growth in InP silicon photonics technology
-
Resolving all prior IP disputes between the companies
IQE plc (AIM: IQE, "IQE" or the "Group"),
the leading global supplier of compound semiconductor wafer products and advanced material solutions, and Tower Semiconductor (NASDAQ/TASE: TSEM), the leading foundry for high-value analog semiconductor solutions,
are pleased to announce a multi-year agreement for the supply of Indium Phosphide (InP) epiwafers for optical connectivity solutions serving AI-driven data centre infrastructure.
IQE's InP epiwafers will be used in several of Tower's advanced silicon photonics platforms for next-generation optical technologies, providing a high-quality supply base for Tower's product roadmap. IQE and Tower's collaboration includes technology for the production of 200Gbs/lane for pluggable transceivers and the prototyping of next-generation 400Gb/lane modulators, as well as other critical applications including optical-circuit-switches for deployment in datacentres. The agreement provides for a minimum purchase commitment by Tower in the first year, a reciprocal supply commitment from IQE, and minimum volume commitments thereafter.
Under a separate agreement, Tower will also provide a broad worldwide and royalty-free license to IQE for porous silicon patents which have been the subject of an IP dispute between the companies, settling all litigation in the matter.
Jutta Meier, Chief Executive Officer of IQE, commented:
"
I am pleased to move forward together with Tower, already the leader in silicon photonics. This agreement reinforces IQE's position within Tier 1 global hyperscale cloud and AI infrastructure
markets. With decades of InP epitaxy expertise and established high-volume manufacturing capability, IQE is primed to support next-generation optical connectivity applications as they scale from innovation to commercial deployment."
Dr. Marco Racanelli, President of Tower
Semiconductor, commented:
"
We are pleased to partner with IQE as a key supplier for our next-generation photonic technologies that add InP high-performance components to our high-volume, mature, silicon photonics platform. The combination will enable products that can deliver both the performance and high volumes required to scale future AI infrastructure capacity
."

ABOUT IQE
http://iqep.com
IQE is the leading global supplier of advanced compound semiconductor wafers and materials solutions that enable a diverse range of applications across:
·
Smart Connected Devices
·
Communications Infrastructure
·
Automotive and Industrial

Aerospace and Security
As a scaled global epitaxy wafer manufacturer, IQE is uniquely positioned in this market which has high barriers to entry. IQE supplies the global market and is enabling customers to innovate at chip and OEM level. By leveraging the Group's intellectual property portfolio including know-how and patents, it produces epitaxy wafers of superior quality, yield and unit economics.
IQE is headquartered in Cardiff UK, with employees across manufacturing locations in the UK, US and Taiwan, and is listed on the AIM Stock Exchange in London.
ABOUT TOWER SEMICONDUCTOR
Tower Semiconductor Ltd. (NASDAQ/TASE: TSEM), the leading foundry of high-value analog semiconductor solutions, provides technology, development, and process platforms for its customers in growing markets such as consumer, industrial, automotive, mobile, infrastructure, medical and aerospace and defense. Tower Semiconductor focuses on creating a positive and sustainable impact on the world through long-term partnerships and its advanced and innovative analog technology offering, comprised of a broad range of customizable process platforms such as SiPho, SiGe, BiCMOS, mixed-signal/CMOS, RF CMOS, CMOS image sensor, non-imaging sensors, displays, integrated power management (BCD and 700V), and MEMS. Tower Semiconductor also provides world-class design enablement for a quick and accurate design cycle as well as process transfer services including development, transfer, and optimization, to IDMs and fabless companies. To provide multi-fab sourcing and extended capacity for its customers, Tower Semiconductor currently owns one operating facility in Israel (200mm), two in the U.S. (200mm), and two in Japan (200mm and 300mm) which it owns through its 51% holdings in TPSCo and shares a 300mm facility in Agrate, Italy with STMicroelectronics. For more information, please visit:
www.towersemi.com
.
Safe Harbor Regarding Forward-Looking Statements
This press release includes forward-looking statements, which are subject to risks and uncertainties. Actual results may vary from those projected or implied by such forward-looking statements. A complete discussion of risks and uncertainties that may affect the accuracy of forward-looking statements included in this press release or which may otherwise affect Tower's business is included under the heading "Risk Factors" in Tower's most recent filings on Forms 20-F, F-3, F-4 and 6-K, as were filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") and the Israel Securities Authority. Tower does not intend to update, and expressly disclaim any obligation to update, the information contained in this release.

latest.title: IQE plc: Director Notification and AGM Update
latest.content:
10 Jun 2026 14:57
RNS Number : 8139H
IQE PLC
10 June 2026
IQE plc
Cardiff, UK
10 June 2026
Director Notification and AGM Update
IQE plc (AIM: IQE), the leading global supplier of compound semiconductor wafer products and advanced material solutions, announces that it has received notification from Victoria Hull, Independent Non-Executive Director, that she does not intend to stand for re-election at the Company's upcoming Annual General Meeting ("AGM"), to be held on 30 June 2026, and accordingly will stand down as a director at the conclusion of the AGM.
Accordingly, the resolution relating to Victoria Hull's re-election, as set out in the Notice of AGM, will be withdrawn and will not be put to shareholders at the AGM.
Victoria Hull has been an Independent Non-Executive Director with the Company for nearly 5 years and currently serves as Chair of the Remuneration Committee and member of the Audit & Risk and Nominations Committees. The Board will undertake a review of committee memberships and her replacement as Chair of the Remuneration Committee in due course.
The Board would like to thank Victoria for her service and wishes her well in her future endeavours as an Independent Non-Executive Director with several other FTSE 100 and FTSE 250 companies.

latest.title: IQE plc: Notice of AGM and Annual Report
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7234G
IQE PLC
03 June 2026
IQE plc
Cardiff, UK
3 June 2026
Notice of Annual General Meeting and Annual Report
IQE plc (AIM: IQE, "IQE" or the "Group"),
the leading global supplier of compound semiconductor wafer products and advanced material solutions
, announces the publication of its 2026 Notice of Annual General Meeting (AGM), which is now available to view on IQE's corporate website at
www.iqep.com/investors/events/agm/
. The AGM will be held at 9:30am on Tuesday 30 June 2026 at Peel Hunt LLP, 7th Floor, 100 Liverpool St, London EC2M 2AT.
Instructions on how shareholders may vote at the AGM and pose questions to the Board are contained in the Notice of AGM. The Company will conduct votes on all resolutions by way of a poll to ensure that the votes of all shareholders are counted, the results of which will be announced during the meeting.
IQE's 2025 Annual Report and Accounts are also now available at
www.iqep.com/investors/results-reports-presentations/
and were posted to shareholders together with the 2026 Notice of Annual General Meeting on 2 June 2026.

company_30s.what_they_do: IQE plc builds and sells high‑performance semiconductor wafers used in advanced electronics such as 5G, automotive sensors, and data‑center processors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company supplies the tiny, high‑quality crystals that power the next generation of chips. With the global shift to AI, autonomous driving and edge computing, the demand for its InP and GaAs wafers is set to grow sharply. IQE’s expertise in wafer‑level fabrication gives it a niche advantage that larger foundries struggle to match.
company_30s.current_state: IQE is in a restructuring phase, trimming costs and focusing on core wafer production. It has recently secured a supply‑agreement with Tower Semiconductor, signalling a steady revenue stream, and its latest FY25 results show a narrowing loss margin. The share price sits well below the valuation implied by its earnings potential.

rns_narrative: The company’s most recent filing announces a new InP wafer supply deal with Tower Semiconductor, a key partner that guarantees orders and reduces revenue volatility.
Earlier this month, IQE released its FY25 financials, showing a significant drop in operating losses and a healthier balance sheet thanks to reduced debt and better cash flow.
Corporate governance updates – a new board slate and a fresh AGM agenda – suggest the company is aligning its leadership with the long‑term turnaround strategy.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – traders are waiting for the first sign of the partnership to translate into earnings.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the Tower tie‑up as a win‑win that locks in high‑margin wafer sales and reduces supply‑chain risk. They also point to the company’s shrinking loss margin as a sign that the cost‑cutting plan is working.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the partnership still leaves IQE exposed to commodity price swings and that the company’s cash burn could resurface if the deal underdelivers.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has shifted from muted skepticism to cautious optimism after the supply agreement, but the community remains largely silent, keeping the setup ripe for a surprise move.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat, indicating no mainstream chatter – a perfect backdrop for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: Tower Semiconductor’s first quarterly delivery under the new InP agreement – a concrete revenue boost that could lift earnings expectations.
Q3 earnings call, where management will discuss the impact of the partnership and any new capacity expansion plans.
A potential price spike if volume exceeds the 20‑day average on a breakout from the 52‑week low, signalling renewed buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume increase coupled with a breakout above the 52‑week low. A clear support at the 20‑day moving average that holds for two consecutive days would confirm a reversal. Any announcement of a new customer or a ramp‑up in wafer capacity would also be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – the price has fallen steeply and the volume has thinned out. The APEX score indicates a tight compression, so a breakout is likely once the new partnership delivers tangible results.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The 86% drawdown puts the share at a massive discount relative to its intrinsic value. With the Tower agreement locking in orders, the mean‑reversion thesis becomes strong. If the price climbs halfway back to the 52‑week high, the upside is roughly double the current level – a 200% potential gain. Historically, deep‑crash signals in tech have delivered 2‑3× returns after a catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the partnership fails to materialise or the company’s cost cuts stall, the share could slide further into the low‑p territory. A sudden increase in raw‑material costs or a new competitor entering the InP market could erode margins. The worst‑case scenario is a 20% additional drop before a rebound, which would still leave the share below its valuation.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30% of the current price if the price falls to the next support level. Potential gain: 200% if the price reaches the 52‑week high. The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1:6, a highly asymmetric play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a volume‑backed breakout above the 52‑week low. Position size: 10–15% of the portfolio, with a stop 5% below the breakout point. Adjust the stop upward as the price climbs to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for the first Tower wafer shipment and Q3 earnings. If the price breaches the 20‑day moving average and volume exceeds the 20‑day average by 30%, consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks. The only terminal risk is a failure of the partnership or a major cost shock. If either materialises, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IQE’s steep fall, coupled with a new high‑margin partnership, sets the stage for a potentially explosive mean‑reversion – a classic deep‑crash contrarian play with high upside and controlled downside.
